text.title: testing-with-assistive-technology Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for testing-with-assistive-technology as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: CSS rule outline: none or outline: 0 applied globally without a replacement focus style.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keyboard users cannot see where they are; fails WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

failure-mode.description: Drag-and-drop with no keyboard alternative (e.g., reorderable list, Kanban board).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fails WCAG 2.5.7 (AA, new in 2.2). Must provide button-driven move alternatives for every drag interaction.

failure-mode.description: Asserting exact SR speech phrases such as "screen reader will say 'invalid'".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Over-specifies phrasing; NVDA and JAWS render the same ARIA differently. Assert on intent (error announced within 1s) not exact string.

failure-mode.description: Closing an a11y bug without linking to the WCAG criterion and pre/post screenshot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reviewers cannot audit drift; regressions are silently reintroduced.
